orchestrator_notes: Enhancement #38. When CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED fires, the orchestrator needs clear remediation guidance. complete_job() already has COMPLETION_BLOCKED enforcement (unread messages + incomplete todos block status change). Research needed: (1) Is COMPLETION_BLOCKED actually working end-to-end? (2) What does the orchestrator see when closeout is blocked? (3) Does the CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED response give enough info for the orchestrator to act? (4) In multi-terminal mode, can the orchestrator give the user a copy-paste prompt to reactivate a stuck agent? (5) In CLI subagent mode, can the orchestrator respawn a Task tool agent to finish the work? The MCP server is passive — it cannot spawn agents. It can only return instructions.

sessions.title: Research: Remediation Protocol Gap Analysis
planned_tasks: Read write_360_memory.py _check_closeout_readiness() — the richer CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED implementation
Read thin_prompt_generator.py build_retirement_prompt() — retirement flow for context exhaustion
Search all COMPLETION_BLOCKED and CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED references across codebase
Synthesize findings across all 6 research tasks
tasks_completed: Read protocol_builder.py _build_ch4_error_handling() — status transitions, no CLOSEOUT
Searched COMPLETION_BLOCKED across entire codebase (7 source files)
Searched CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED across entire codebase (10 source files)
Synthesized gap analysis across all 6 categories
deviations: Discovered TWO separate CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED implementations: project_closeout.py (simpler, raises exception) and write_360_memory.py (richer, returns dict). Handover only mentioned project_closeout.py.
docs/ORCHESTRATOR.md contains detailed CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED handling patterns that are NOT injected into any runtime prompt — this is documentation-only knowledge.

response_contains.message: COMPLETION_BLOCKED: Complete all TODO items and read all messages before calling complete_job()
response_contains.context_reasons: Up to 2 entries: unread messages with IDs + incomplete TODO names

details.agent_pre_warning: Phase 4 of _generate_agent_protocol() warns: 'System will REJECT your completion. Response will list specific blockers.' But does NOT name the error code 'COMPLETION_BLOCKED' explicitly.
details.remediation_instructions_in_response: YES — tells agent to call receive_messages() and lists up to 5 message IDs. Names up to 5 incomplete TODO items.
details.staleness_mechanism: NONE — no TTL, no heartbeat timeout, no automatic decommission for stuck agents
details.mcp_http_handling: api/endpoints/mcp_http.py line 992: COMPLETION_BLOCKED validation errors are logged at DEBUG instead of ERROR to avoid console pollution. Error still returned to agent in MCP format.

gaps: No staleness/timeout mechanism — agent can stay 'working' forever with no automatic cleanup
Phase 4 protocol does not name the error code 'COMPLETION_BLOCKED' — agent sees it for the first time when rejected (minor, since the error message is self-explanatory)
No retry guidance in error response — doesn't say 'fix these issues then call complete_job() again'

response_contains.blockers: list of {agent_id, agent_name, status, job_id, messages_waiting}
response_contains.hint: Resolve all blockers or pass force=true to auto-decommission remaining agents.

project_closeout_py.richness: BASIC — only status and messages_waiting per agent

write_360_memory_py.caller: write_360_memory() at line 312, only when entry_type='project_completion' and author_job_id is provided

response_contains.blockers: list with issue_type differentiation: still_working, unread_messages, incomplete_todos, orchestrator_incomplete_todos
response_contains.action_required: Resolve all blockers before closeout. Use report_error() if unable to resolve.

write_360_memory_py.richness: GOOD — per-agent issue type, counts, and item names

details.force_decommission: project_closeout.py supports force=true which calls _force_decommission_agents() to set all active agents to 'decommissioned' status

gaps: project_closeout.py CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED is much less rich than write_360_memory.py — no issue_type, no todo details, no summary counts
Neither implementation includes per-blocker REMEDIATION STEPS (e.g., 'send message to agent X asking for status', 'call receive_messages(agent_id=X) to drain their queue')
Neither implementation generates a copy-paste reactivation prompt for multi-terminal mode
The 'action_required' field is generic ('Resolve all blockers...') — not per-blocker actionable instructions
No differentiation between 'agent is actively working and making progress' vs 'agent is stuck/dead/context-exhausted'

cli_mode_remediation.how: The orchestrator CAN call receive_messages(agent_id=X), report_progress(job_id=X), and complete_job(job_id=X) on behalf of any agent in the same tenant. The retirement prompt (build_retirement_prompt) already demonstrates this full pattern: drain messages, mark todos as skipped, force-complete. Nothing in the auth/tenant model prevents cross-agent operations within the same tenant.
gaps: CRITICAL: The CLI implementation prompt (_build_claude_code_execution_prompt) does NOT tell the orchestrator it can act on behalf of stuck agents. Section 7 'When You're Done' just says 'Ensure all have status=complete' with no fallback if they don't.
CRITICAL: No CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED handling instructions in the CLI prompt at all — the orchestrator has no idea what to do if closeout fails
The retirement prompt has the full remediation pattern but it only fires on context exhaustion, not on normal closeout failure
The orchestrator could respawn a Task tool subagent to finish work, but is never told this is an option

CRITICAL: The multi-terminal prompt (_build_multi_terminal_orchestrator_prompt) Section 5 'Project Closeout' only covers the happy path ('When all agents show status complete'). No handling for when they don't.
No instructions for generating a paste-able reactivation prompt for the user
System does NOT track which terminal an agent is running in — orchestrator cannot say 'go to terminal 3'
docs/ORCHESTRATOR.md has the CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED handling pattern (send messages to agents by issue_type) but this documentation is NOT injected into any runtime prompt
Section 4 'Handling Agent Issues' mentions spawning fresh agents for tester problems but not for closeout remediation

A_agent_self_remediation.assessment: Mostly working. Agents are warned about completion requirements in Phase 4 protocol. COMPLETION_BLOCKED error response is actionable with message IDs and TODO names. Only minor gap: error code name not mentioned in advance, no explicit 'retry after fixing' instruction.
items: Phase 4 does not name 'COMPLETION_BLOCKED' error code (minor)
Error response does not say 'fix these then retry complete_job()' (minor)
No staleness timeout for agents that never attempt completion (systemic)

B_orchestrator_remediation.assessment: Major gap. Neither the CLI nor multi-terminal orchestrator prompt includes ANY CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED handling instructions. The orchestrator has no idea what to do when closeout fails. The retirement prompt demonstrates the full remediation pattern but only fires on context exhaustion.
items: CLI prompt: no CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED handling, no 'act on behalf of agent' instructions
Multi-terminal prompt: no CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED handling, no 'tell user to reactivate agent' instructions
docs/ORCHESTRATOR.md has the pattern but it's not injected into prompts
Retirement prompt has full pattern but wrong trigger (context exhaustion, not closeout failure)

C_response_richness.assessment: Split quality. write_360_memory.py has good response richness (issue_type differentiation, todo names, summary). project_closeout.py is basic (just status + messages_waiting). Neither includes per-blocker remediation steps or reactivation prompts.
items: project_closeout.py CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED lacks issue_type, todo details, summary
Neither response includes per-blocker suggested actions
Neither response includes reactivation prompt for multi-terminal mode
No differentiation between 'actively progressing' vs 'stuck/dead' agents

D_missing_features.assessment: Several useful features are missing but not critical for MVP.
items: No staleness/timeout mechanism for agents that never complete
No per-blocker remediation steps in CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED response
No reactivation prompt generation for multi-terminal mode
No 'last_activity_at' or heartbeat tracking to differentiate stuck vs active agents
Orchestrator prompts don't include explicit CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED handling section

findings.assessment_rationale: The enforcement gates (COMPLETION_BLOCKED and CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED) work correctly. The agent self-remediation path for COMPLETION_BLOCKED is good. But there is a CRITICAL gap: orchestrators in BOTH execution modes receive NO instructions about handling CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED. The system blocks the closeout correctly but then leaves the orchestrator with no idea how to recover. The retirement prompt proves the remediation pattern works — it just needs to be exposed during normal closeout flow too.

proposed_fixes.name: Add CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED handling to CLI orchestrator prompt
proposed_fixes.description: Add a new section to _build_claude_code_execution_prompt() after 'When You're Done' that handles the case where get_workflow_status shows agents not complete. Instructions: (1) check each agent's status, (2) for stuck agents: drain their messages via receive_messages(agent_id=X), mark their todos as skipped via report_progress(job_id=X), then force-complete via complete_job(job_id=X), (3) for actively working agents: wait and check again. This mirrors the retirement prompt pattern but for normal closeout.
proposed_fixes.rationale: Highest impact fix. The orchestrator already CAN act on behalf of agents (tenant auth allows it), it just doesn't KNOW it can. This is purely an instruction gap.

proposed_fixes.name: Add CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED handling to multi-terminal orchestrator prompt
proposed_fixes.description: Add a new section to _build_multi_terminal_orchestrator_prompt() after 'Project Closeout' for when agents are NOT complete. Instructions: (1) send message to stuck agents asking them to complete, (2) if agent terminal is closed/dead, tell user to open a new terminal and paste a continuation prompt, (3) provide the user with the agent's job_id and a get_agent_mission() call to paste. Since the orchestrator cannot act on behalf of agents in multi-terminal mode (agents are in separate terminals), the remediation is user-directed.
proposed_fixes.rationale: Critical for multi-terminal UX. Without this, the orchestrator just says 'all agents should be complete' and has no fallback.

proposed_fixes.name: Enrich project_closeout.py CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED response to match write_360_memory.py
proposed_fixes.description: Upgrade _check_agent_readiness() in project_closeout.py to include issue_type differentiation (still_working, unread_messages, incomplete_todos), todo item names, and a summary dict — matching the richer format in write_360_memory.py's _check_closeout_readiness(). Also add per-blocker suggested_action field.
proposed_fixes.rationale: Consistency fix. Two CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED paths should return the same quality of data. The richer response enables orchestrators to make better remediation decisions.

proposed_fixes.name: Add per-blocker remediation steps to CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED responses
proposed_fixes.description: In both CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED paths, add a 'suggested_action' field per blocker: for still_working -> 'Send message asking for status or call complete_job(job_id=X) to force-complete', for unread_messages -> 'Call receive_messages(agent_id=X) to drain queue', for incomplete_todos -> 'Call report_progress(job_id=X) to mark todos as skipped'. These are mode-agnostic suggestions the orchestrator can adapt to its execution mode.
proposed_fixes.rationale: Makes CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED responses self-documenting. Even without prompt changes, an intelligent orchestrator could follow these per-blocker suggestions.

proposed_fixes.name: Add agent staleness tracking (last_activity_at field)
proposed_fixes.description: Add a last_activity_at timestamp to AgentExecution, updated on every report_progress(), receive_messages(), and send_message() call. This allows CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED to differentiate 'agent is actively working (last_activity 30s ago)' vs 'agent appears stuck (last_activity 10min ago)'. Include staleness_minutes in CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED blocker entries.
proposed_fixes.rationale: Nice-to-have for distinguishing stuck vs active agents. Requires DB migration. Lower priority than prompt/response fixes. Could be deferred to a future enhancement.

sessions.notes_for_next: IMPLEMENTATION PRIORITIES for 0800b: (1) Add CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED handling section to _build_claude_code_execution_prompt() in thin_prompt_generator.py — this is the highest-impact fix, ~40 LOC. Mirror the retirement prompt's drain-and-complete pattern. (2) Add CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED handling section to _build_multi_terminal_orchestrator_prompt() — user-directed remediation, ~35 LOC. (3) Enrich _check_agent_readiness() in project_closeout.py to match write_360_memory.py quality — add issue_type, todo details, summary. (4) Add per-blocker suggested_action to both CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED response paths. (5) SKIP staleness tracking (requires DB migration, lower priority). Total estimated: ~155 LOC across 3 files. All backward-compatible, no DB migrations needed for fixes 1-4.
sessions.summary: The COMPLETION_BLOCKED gate works well — agents are pre-warned and the error response is actionable. The CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED gate enforcement works but has a CRITICAL orchestrator instruction gap: neither CLI nor multi-terminal orchestrator prompts include ANY handling for when closeout is blocked. The system correctly blocks premature closeout but then provides no recovery path. The retirement prompt (build_retirement_prompt) already demonstrates the full remediation pattern (drain messages, skip todos, force-complete) but it only fires on context exhaustion, not on normal closeout failure. Four recommended fixes: (1) CLI prompt CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED handling section, (2) multi-terminal prompt CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED handling section, (3) enrich project_closeout.py response to match write_360_memory.py, (4) add per-blocker suggested_action fields. All backward-compatible, ~155 LOC total, no DB migrations.

sessions.title: Implement: Remediation Protocol Enhancements
planned_tasks: Fix 1: Add CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED handling to _build_claude_code_execution_prompt() in thin_prompt_generator.py (~40 LOC)
Fix 2: Add CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED handling to _build_multi_terminal_orchestrator_prompt() in thin_prompt_generator.py (~35 LOC)
Fix 3: Enrich _check_agent_readiness() in project_closeout.py to match write_360_memory.py quality (~50 LOC)
Fix 4: Add per-blocker suggested_action to both CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED response paths (~30 LOC)
tasks_completed: Read 0800a findings and build_retirement_prompt() reference pattern
Fix 1: Added CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED recovery section to _build_claude_code_execution_prompt() — drain messages, update todos, force-complete pattern (mirroring retirement prompt STEP 2)
Fix 2: Added CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED recovery section to _build_multi_terminal_orchestrator_prompt() — user-directed remediation with send_message + reactivation prompt for closed terminals
Fix 3: Enriched _check_agent_readiness() in project_closeout.py — added AgentTodoItem query, issue_type field, incomplete_todo_count/names, summary dict appended to blockers

Fix 4: Added suggested_action field to all 4 blocker types in write_360_memory.py (still_working, unread_messages, incomplete_todos, orchestrator_incomplete_todos) and project_closeout.py (integrated into Fix 3)
Updated existing closeout readiness gate tests to handle enriched response format (new todo queries + _summary entry)
Run full test suite: 1390 passed, 0 failed
deviations: Fix 3: Simplified issue_type logic — since _check_agent_readiness already filters out complete and decommissioned agents, all remaining agents are always 'still_working'. Made suggested_action context-aware (includes drain/todo steps only when relevant) rather than branching on issue_type.
Fix 3: Summary is appended as a trailing {'_summary': {...}} entry in the blockers list rather than a separate field, to maintain backward compatibility with callers that iterate blockers.
Updated 3 existing tests in test_closeout_readiness_gate.py to handle the new AgentTodoItem query and _summary entry — these were not net-new tests but necessary mock adjustments.
sessions.summary: All 4 fixes implemented across 3 source files. CLI orchestrator prompt now includes drain-and-complete recovery flow for non-complete agents. Multi-terminal orchestrator prompt now includes send-message-first + user-directed reactivation fallback. project_closeout.py _check_agent_readiness() now returns enriched blockers with issue_type, todo details, and per-blocker suggested_action. write_360_memory.py _check_closeout_readiness() blocker entries now include suggested_action. All backward-compatible, no DB migrations. 1390 tests passing.

chain_summary: Chain complete. CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED enforcement gates were already working; the gap was that orchestrators received no recovery instructions when closeout was blocked. Fixed by: (1) adding drain-and-complete recovery to CLI orchestrator prompt, (2) adding user-directed recovery to multi-terminal orchestrator prompt, (3) enriching project_closeout.py CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED response with issue_type + todo details + summary, (4) adding per-blocker suggested_action to both CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED response paths.
